title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Remote work has (0) from a rare privilege to a mainstream practice in just a few years. While working from home was once limited to certain professions, advances in technology have made it possible for millions of employees to perform their jobs from anywhere with an internet connection. The global pandemic (1) this trend dramatically, forcing companies to adapt overnight. Many workers discovered significant benefits, including the elimination of stressful commutes and greater flexibility to (2) work and family responsibilities. Employers found that productivity often increased when staff worked remotely, challenging traditional assumptions about the need for office (3). However, remote work also presents challenges. Some employees struggle with isolation and find it difficult to (4) boundaries between their professional and personal lives. Communication can suffer when colleagues are not physically present, and building team spirit requires more deliberate (5). Companies are now experimenting with hybrid models that combine the best of both approaches. Workers might spend some days in the office for collaboration and meetings while working from home on tasks that require (6) concentration. The future of work is still (7) shape, but it seems clear that the traditional nine-to-five office job will never be quite the same again. Both employers and employees must continue to adapt as we work out the best ways to balance flexibility with (8) and connection.

example.explanation: 'Evolved from... to' is the correct expression to describe a gradual and significant change.

questions.explanation: 'Accelerated this trend' is the correct collocation to describe that something accelerated a trend.
'Balance work and family' is the standard collocation to describe work-life balance.
'Office presence' is the correct expression to refer to physical presence in the office.
'Set boundaries' is the correct placement to establish clear boundaries.
'Deliberate effort' is the correct collocation to describe a conscious and intentional effort.
'Deep concentration' is the most natural collocation to describe deep concentration.
'Taking shape' is a fixed expression that means that something is beginning to take shape.
'Productivity' is the most appropriate term in the work context to measure performance.
